title: Gas assisted chemical injection system
abstract: A system and method for dispensing an injection material 12 to an injection location 16. The injection material 12 is stored in a tank 14 and disposed to parallel piped vessels 24 1 , 24 2 . An orifice 36 is disposed in piping connected to discharge ends of the vessels 24 1 , 24 2 , and has an opening 37 with a designated cross sectional area. A high pressure fluid drives the injection material 12 to the injection location 16; the pressure of the high pressure fluid is regulated with a pressure control valve 52. Valves 28 1 , 28 2 , and valves 38 1 , 38 2 in the inlet and discharge piping 18, 30 connected to the vessels 24 1 , 24 2 are selectively opened and closed so that injection material 12 flows to and from the vessels 24 1 , 24 2 . Vessels 24 1 , 24 2 vent to a recycle/recovery system 64 to reduce vessel pressure enabling refilling with injection material 12. Valves 52 1 , 52 2 , and valves 58 1 , 58 2 in the pressurizing lines and depressurizing lines 48, 60 are selectively opened and closed to depressurize and refill the vessels 24 1 , 24 2 to have continuous chemical injection to the injection location 16.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is. 1. A method of dispensing an injection material comprising: a. directing injection material 12 into a first vessel 24i; b. pressurizing the first vessel 241 to a specified pressure; c. discharging a designated amount of the injection material 12 from the first vessel 241 ; d. directing the injection material 12 from the first vessel 241 to an injection location 16; e. depressurizing the first vessel 24i; and characterized by, f. repeating steps (a) - (e) using a second vessel 24 2 . 2. The method of Claim 1, further characterized by regulating a flow of the injection material 12 directed from the first vessel 241 with an orifice 36i having an opening with a designated cross sectional area. 3. The method of Claims 1 or 2, further characterized by directing injection material 12 from the second vessel 24 2 to the injection location 16 while suspending the step of directing injection material 12 from the first vessel 241 to the injection location 16. 4. The method of any of Claims 1 - 3, further characterized by directing injection material 12 to the second vessel 24 2 while suspending the step of directing injection material 12 into the first vessel 24i. 5. The method of Claim 4, characterized in that an inlet piping circuit 18 provides fluid communication between a tank 14 in which injection material 12 is stored and the first and second vessels 24i, 24 2 , and a discharge circuit 30 provides fluid communication between the first and second vessels 24i, 24 2 and the injection location 16. 6. The method of Claim 5, characterized in that valves 28i, 28 2 , 38i, 38 2 are provided in the inlet piping circuit 18 and discharge circuit 30 that are selectively opened and closed to selectively direct injection material 12 from and to the first and second vessels 24i, 24 2 . 7. The method of Claim 6, further characterized by using a controller 70 to direct command signals to actuators 29i, 29 2 , 39 l5 39 2 mounted to the valves for opening and closing the valves 281, 282, 381, 382. 8. The method of any of Claims 1 - 7, further characterized by venting pressurized fluid from one of the first or second vessel 24i, 24 2 to a recycle/recovery system 64. 9. The method of any of Claims 1 - 8, characterized in that the injection material 12 flows to the injection location 16 continuously and uninterrupted. 10. The method of any of Claims 1 - 9, further characterized by venting pressurized fluid between the first and second vessel 24i, 24 2 . 11. A method of dispensing an injection material 12 comprising: a. directing injection material 12 into a vessel 24; b. directing pressurized fluid across a pressure control valve 54 and to the vessel to pressurize the vessel to a specified pressure; c. discharging a designated amount of the injection material 12 from the vessel to an injection location 16; and characterized by, d. controlling the amount of injection material 12 flowing from the vessel at the designated amount by flowing the injection material 12 through an orifice 36 having an opening with a particular cross sectional area in conjunction with maintaining a pressure of the pressurized fluid directed across the pressure control valve 54 at the specified pressure. 12. The method of Claim 11, characterized in that the vessel comprises a first vessel 24i, the method further comprising depressurizing the first vessel 24i; and repeating steps (a) - (d) using a second vessel 24 2 . 13. A system for dispensing injection material 12 characterized by: a vessel that selectively contains the injection material 12; a pressure control valve 52 having in inlet in communication with a pressure source 46, and an outlet in communication with the vessel 24 having a varying pressure so that pressure in the vessel is at a designated value; 

 a piping circuit 30 having ends connected to the vessel and to an injection location 16 so that the vessel and injection location 16 are in communication through the piping circuit 30; an orifice 36 in the piping circuit 30 having an opening 37 with a particular cross sectional area; and a controller 70 in signal communication with the pressure control valve 52 and a flowmeter 40 in the piping circuit 30 that provides commands to the pressure control valve 52 based on a comparison of a rate of flow of the injection material 12 measured by the flowmeter 40 and a designated rate of flow of the injection material 12. 14. The system of Claim 13, characterized in that the particular cross sectional area of the opening 37 in the orifice 36 is strategically sized, so that the rate of flow of injection material 12 is substantially the same as the designated amount of injection material 12 is metered to the injection location 16 when pressure at the pressure control valve 52 outlet is substantially at the designated value. 15. The system of Claim 13, characterized in that the vessel comprises a first vessel 241, and where the system comprises a second vessel 24 2 . 16. The system of Claim 15, further characterized by inlet piping 18 in communication with inlets formed in the first and second vessel 24i, 24 2 and also in communication with a tank 14 in which injection material 12 is stored. 17. The system of any of Claims 13 - 16, further characterized by a pressure discharge circuit 56 having an end in communication with the vessel, and a distal end in communication with a recycle/recovery system 64. 18. The system of any of Claims 13 - 17, characterized in that the injection location 16 comprises a position that is selected from the group consisting of a portion of a processing facility and a storage tank. 19. The system of any of Claims 13 - 18, where the injection material 12 alters a physical property of compounds in the injection location 16.
